Boston July 4th Trip

July 2 – 5, 2026

Sightseeing
Meal
Special Event
On the Water
Mass
ThursdayJuly 2 — Arrival + Fireworks
1 pm
🚗 Home → Boston
Depart Home
4.5–5 hrs on I-95 with Thursday afternoon traffic. Pick up Mandy along the way. Arrive Ritz-Carlton Boston Common ~6pm. Check in, freshen up, then head to dinner.
8 pm
Dinner
Davio's Northern Italian Steakhouse — Seaport
26 Fan Pier — harbor views, open kitchen, handmade pasta, prime steaks. 8pm reservation confirmed. We'll catch the fireworks right from our table as we dine.
During dinner
🎆 Fireworks
Boston Fireworks — from Davio's
Watch the show from our table as we dine — no crowds, no scrambling for a spot.
FridayJuly 3 — Ranger Tour + Evening Cruise
9:30 am
Breakfast
Tatte Bakery & Café — Downtown Crossing
Israeli-inspired pastries, shakshuka, excellent coffee. 201 Washington St — about a 3-min walk from the hotel. Then walk to Faneuil Hall — about 10 minutes on foot.
11 am
🎟️ NPS Ranger Tour
Ranger-Guided Freedom Trail Tour — from Faneuil Hall
The National Park Service ranger walk ("The Freedom Trail: Enemies to This Country") — about 75 minutes, starting inside the Faneuil Hall Visitor Center and ending at the Old North Church in the North End. Reservation confirmed — arrive by ~10:30am.

🎧 Want the full self-guided trail too?

The ranger tour covers the downtown stops. For the rest — and to listen on our own — here's the complete NPS audio tour: all 23 stops with a blurb and full detail for each.
~12:45 pm
Lunch — North End
Quattro Ristorante (Hanover St)
The tour ends right in the North End, so lunch is a few steps away. Casual spot — rotisserie, brick-oven pizza, fresh seafood. Fast, broad menu, no reservation needed. Cannoli from Mike's Pastry (300 Hanover) on the way out.
2 – 5 pm
Freedom Trail — On Our Own
Continue the trail: North End → Charlestown
Pick up where the ranger tour ends (Old North Church) and follow the brick line to Copp's Hill Burying Ground, then across the bridge to the USS Constitution and the Bunker Hill Monument. Use the guide below — same 23 stops, map, and audio — for each stop. About 1.5 miles at an easy pace. Heads up: Boston is under an Extreme Heat Watch July 1–4, with highs near 100°F and heat index up to 110°F on Friday. If it's too much by this point, swap in the ICA (Institute of Contemporary Art) on the Seaport waterfront — air-conditioned, on the water — or head back to the Ritz to cool off before the cruise.

🧱 Our self-guided companion

The same Freedom Trail guide — all 23 stops with the NPS map, a blurb and full detail for each, and the audio tour.
5:30 – 7:30 pm
⛵ Evening Cruise
Boston Harbor Cruise — from Long Wharf
A ~2-hour evening cruise from Long Wharf (near the Aquarium) — Boston skyline and harbor islands in the long summer light. In July, sunset isn't until around 8:15pm, so expect plenty of daylight with golden light toward the end rather than a dark night out on the water.
7:45 pm
Dinner
The Daily Catch — Atlantic Ave
Right near where the cruise drops us — on Atlantic Ave. Sicilian seafood, squid ink pasta, calamari. 7:45pm reservation confirmed. Cash only — bring cash.
SaturdayJuly 4 — A Relaxed Independence Day
10 am
Late Breakfast
Flour Bakery + Café
Sleep in. Legendary sticky buns and egg sandwiches. Fort Point or South End. Loved Tatte on Friday? Happy to go back — it's closer to the hotel.
11:30 am
Gardens
Boston Public Garden — Morning Stroll
Flat, beautiful, full of benches. Swan boats, the lagoon footbridge, Make Way for Ducklings statues. 45–60 min of pure relaxation.
1 pm
Lunch — Back Bay
Parish Café (Boylston St)
Sandwiches by the city's top chefs, fast and casual, broad menu. No reservation needed. 5-min walk from the Garden.
3 pm
Free Afternoon
Rest & Freshen Up
Head back to the Ritz to recharge. St. Anthony Shrine is a 12-min walk from the hotel — Mass is at 4pm.
4 pm
✝️ Mass
St. Anthony Shrine — 4pm Mass
Franciscan shrine at 100 Arch St, Downtown Crossing — 12-min walk from the Ritz. Done by ~5pm, plenty of time to head to the fireworks dinner.
6:30 pm
Dinner + Fireworks
Scampo — The Liberty Hotel
Reserved at Scampo. After dinner, watch the Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular from the Liberty's fireworks watch party — right near the Esplanade.
SundayJuly 5 — Open Morning, Leave When Ready
Morning
☀️ Easy Morning
Leisurely Start
Sleep in, linger over hotel breakfast, stroll Newbury Street. Head to the Gardner Museum when it opens at 11am.
11 am
Art
Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum
Opens at 11am. A Venetian palazzo wrapped around an indoor garden, plus the famous unsolved 1990 art heist (empty frames still on the walls). About 1.5–2 hrs. ~$20/person. Reserve timed tickets in advance or pick up upon arrival.
1 pm
Farewell Lunch
Bistro du Midi — or Open
French bistro overlooking the Public Garden — mussels, salads, croque madame. ~8-min walk from the Ritz. One option, not a must — happy to land wherever looks good when we're ready.
Whenever
🚗 Boston → Home
Head Home
Boston to Home. Drop off Mandy on the way.
Boston July 4th Trip · July 2–5
Friday · July 3

The Freedom Trail
